name: Salesforce
description: Salesforce is a customer relationship management (CRM) and sales platform.
access_note: Developers can obtain API keys by setting up a Connected App and configuring OAuth 2.0, but may require a paid plan or admin approval.
api_breadth: full CRUD on core objects

name: HubSpot
description: HubSpot is a CRM and sales platform that enables businesses to manage their customer relationships and sales processes.
access_note: Developers can obtain API keys by creating a private app, but some features may require a paid plan or admin approval.
api_breadth: full CRUD on core objects

name: Pipedrive
description: Pipedrive is a CRM and sales platform that provides API access for integrations and customizations.
access_note: Developers can obtain API credentials by signing up for a free account or sandbox environment.
api_breadth: full CRUD on core objects

name: Attio
description: Attio is a CRM that provides a public REST API for developers to build custom integrations and automate customer relationship workflows.
access_note: Developers can obtain API credentials by creating a new integration in their workspace settings, but may need to contact support for a developer account.
api_breadth: full CRUD on core objects

name: Twenty
description: Twenty is an open-source CRM that provides building blocks for a custom CRM to meet complex business needs.
access_note: Developers can create an API key in Settings → API & Webhooks → + Create key.
api_breadth: full CRUD on all core objects

name: Podio
description: Podio is a CRM and sales platform that provides a programmable interface to all its functionality.
access_note: Developers can get an API key by signing in or signing up with Podio.
api_breadth: full CRUD on all core objects

name: Zoho CRM
description: Zoho CRM is a customer relationship management platform that provides APIs for integration with third-party applications.
access_note: Developers can obtain credentials through the Zoho Developer Console.
api_breadth: full CRUD on core objects
mcp_note: CData MCP Server for Zoho CRM

name: Close
description: Close is a CRM and sales platform that provides API access for integrations and automation.
access_note: Developers can obtain API keys in their Close account under Settings → Developer → API Keys.
api_breadth: full CRUD on core objects

name: Copper
description: Copper is a CRM and sales platform that provides a RESTful interface with JSON-formatted responses to access most Copper resources.
access_note: Developers can generate an API key in the Copper web app.
api_breadth: full access to most Copper resources

name: DealCloud
description: DealCloud is a CRM and sales platform that provides a comprehensive API for custom integrations and automation.
access_note: Developers can obtain API credentials by enabling API access in their user group permissions and generating an API key in their profile.
api_breadth: full CRUD on core objects

name: Zendesk
description: Zendesk is a support and helpdesk platform that provides APIs for ticketing, help center, live chat, and more.
access_note: Free 14-day trial has full API access, and Zendesk sponsors non-expiring dev instances for integration builders.
api_breadth: full CRUD on core objects
revision_note: corrected after human docs check (see manual_checks.json)

name: Intercom
description: Intercom is a support and helpdesk platform that provides APIs for developers to build integrations and custom applications.
access_note: Developers can obtain credentials by creating a free developer workspace and app.
api_breadth: full CRUD on core objects

name: Freshdesk
description: Freshdesk is a support and helpdesk application
access_note: API access is available from paid plans onwards
api_breadth: full CRUD on core objects

name: Front
description: Front is a helpdesk and support platform that allows users to manage customer conversations and automate workflows.
access_note: Developers can create a free developer account to access credentials.
api_breadth: full CRUD on core objects

name: Pylon
description: Pylon is a B2B customer support and customer operations platform.
access_note: Developers can get access to Pylon's API by contacting support staff, but only Admin users can create API tokens.
api_breadth: full CRUD on core objects

name: LiveAgent
description: LiveAgent is an integrated customer support software, ticketing system, live chat, social helpdesk and voice helpdesk.
access_note: Developers can get credentials by logging in as an admin and navigating to Configuration > System > API.
api_breadth: full CRUD on core objects

name: Plain
description: Plain is an API-first customer infrastructure platform for B2B SaaS that provides a support stack with a GraphQL API.
access_note: API keys are created for Machine Users in workspace settings; free trial available.
api_breadth: full API-UI parity
mcp_note: Plain has native MCP server support
revision_note: corrected in verification fix pass (see verification.json pass1)

name: Help Scout
description: Help Scout is a support and helpdesk platform that provides tools for developers to integrate with AI agents.
access_note: Developers can create a free account and obtain API credentials, but some features may require a paid plan.
api_breadth: full CRUD on core objects

name: Gorgias
description: Gorgias is a support and helpdesk platform that provides an open REST API for reading and writing data programmatically.
access_note: Developers can obtain API credentials by registering on the Developer Portal and creating a trial account, but may need to contact support to transform it into a developer account.
api_breadth: full CRUD on core objects

name: Gladly
description: Gladly is a customer service platform that provides APIs for integrating with external systems and automating workflows.
access_note: Developers can obtain API tokens by creating an account and navigating to the API tokens section in the Gladly UI.
api_breadth: full CRUD on core objects
mcp_note: Gladly MCP Server for AI Agents

name: Slack
description: Slack is a communication platform that allows users to interact with each other and with AI agents through its API.
access_note: Developers can obtain credentials by creating an app on the Slack API website.
api_breadth: full CRUD on all core objects

name: Twilio
description: Twilio provides cloud communications APIs for messaging, voice, video, email and other channels.
access_note: Developers can sign up for a free trial account on Twilio.com and create API keys in the Console without contacting sales.
api_breadth: full CRUD on all core communication objects
mcp_note: Twilio Model Context Protocol (MCP) server – https://www.twilio.com/docs/ai/mcp

name: Zoho Cliq
description: Zoho Cliq is a team chat and collaboration platform for channels, threads, reminders and status updates.
access_note: Developers register an app in the Zoho API console to obtain OAuth client ID/secret; no paid tier required.
api_breadth: full CRUD on chat objects (channels, messages, reminders, users)

name: Lark
description: Lark is a workplace communication and collaboration platform.
access_note: Developers obtain an App ID and App Secret from the Lark Developer Console.
api_breadth: broad set of workplace collaboration APIs

name: Pumble
description: Pumble is an all‑in‑one team communication app for messaging, file sharing and video conferencing.
access_note: Install the API addon in a workspace and generate an API key via the UI; available on all plans including the free tier.
api_breadth: full CRUD on messages, channels, reactions and user interactions

name: Discord
description: Discord is a communications platform that provides chat, voice, and community features via a REST API.
access_note: Create an app in the Discord Developer Portal to obtain a bot token or OAuth2 credentials for free.
api_breadth: full CRUD on core Discord objects (guilds, channels, messages, users)

name: Telegram
description: Telegram provides a cloud‑based messaging platform with a Bot API that lets developers create bots and interact with chats via HTTP requests.
access_note: Developers obtain a bot token from BotFather or an api_id/api_hash by registering an app at https://my.telegram.org/apps – free and without sales approval.
api_breadth: full CRUD on core messaging objects (messages, chats, users, etc.)
mcp_note: sparfenyuk/mcp-telegram (GitHub) – a community MCP server bridging Telegram to the Model Context Protocol

name: WhatsApp Business
description: Provides a programmable API for businesses to send and receive WhatsApp messages.
access_note: Free test number + temporary access token on signup; production messaging needs a permanent system-user token and Meta business verification.
api_breadth: full messaging (send, receive, templates, media)
revision_note: corrected after human docs check (see manual_checks.json)

name: Aircall
description: Aircall provides a cloud‑based phone system with a public REST API for managing calls, users, contacts, numbers and more.
access_note: API ID and API token are generated in the Aircall dashboard (Company Settings → API Keys) without needing sales approval.
api_breadth: full CRUD on core objects (users, calls, contacts, numbers, teams, tags, webhooks, etc.)
mcp_note: Aircall MCP server available via viaSocket (https://viasocket.com/mcp/aircall).

name: Vonage
description: Vonage provides communication APIs for voice, video, messaging, and network features.
access_note: Sign up for a free account on the Developer Dashboard to obtain an API key and secret; free trial credit is provided.
api_breadth: full CRUD across voice, video, messaging, and network APIs

name: Google Ads
description: The Google Ads API is a programmatic interface for managing Google Ads accounts and campaigns.
access_note: Developers must apply for a 22‑character developer token via the API Center of a Google Ads manager account, which is reviewed before use.
api_breadth: full CRUD on campaigns, ads, keywords and reporting
mcp_note: Google Ads MCP server (Model Context Protocol) – official server source on GitHub

name: Meta Ads
description: Meta Ads is a platform that allows developers to automate ad creation, management, and reporting across Facebook, Instagram, Messenger, and WhatsApp.
access_note: Developers need to create a Meta developer app, generate access tokens, and obtain necessary permissions to access the API.
api_breadth: full CRUD on ad accounts and campaigns

name: LinkedIn Ads
description: The LinkedIn Ads app helps businesses grow by building technology to reach, engage, and convert professional audiences at scale.
access_note: Developers can apply for access through the Developer Portal, but approval is required.
api_breadth: full CRUD on core objects

name: GoHighLevel
description: GoHighLevel is a CRM and marketing automation platform that lets businesses manage contacts, communications, appointments, and payments.
access_note: Basic API access is included with Starter/Unlimited plans; OAuth and advanced access require the paid Agency Pro plan.
api_breadth: full CRUD on core objects (contacts, conversations, calendar, payments, etc.)
blocker: OAuth requires paid Agency Pro plan

name: Mailchimp
description: Mailchimp provides a marketing platform for creating, sending, and tracking email campaigns and managing audiences.
access_note: Developers generate an API key in their Mailchimp account settings (free accounts can do this) or use OAuth 2 for user‑delegated access.
api_breadth: full CRUD on core marketing objects (audiences, contacts, campaigns, automation, etc.)
mcp_note: Apify Mailchimp MCP Server (https://apify.com/engaging_image/mailchimp-mcp-server) and open‑source GitHub repo (https://github.com/LokiMCPUniverse/mailchimp-mcp-server)

name: Klaviyo
description: Klaviyo provides marketing automation APIs for managing profiles, events, lists, campaigns and more.
access_note: After signing up, users can create private or public API keys in Settings → API keys without contacting sales.
api_breadth: full CRUD on core marketing objects (profiles, events, lists, campaigns, etc.)

name: systeme.io
description: Systeme.io is an all‑in‑one platform for building sales funnels, email marketing, online courses and other digital business tools.
access_note: Log in to a Systeme.io account, go to Settings → Public API keys and create a key; no paid plan required.
api_breadth: CRUD on contacts, tags, subscriptions, newsletters and related objects
mcp_note: Model Context Protocol (MCP) server – help.systeme.io article on using MCP

name: Pinterest
description: Provides an API to create and manage Pins, Boards, ads, product catalogs, and analytics on Pinterest.
access_note: Create an app on the Pinterest developer portal; trial access is granted after approval and standard access requires a review video.
api_breadth: full CRUD on content, ads, catalogs plus analytics
blocker: App review and video demo required for standard access

name: Threads
description: Provides an API for creating, publishing, and retrieving Threads posts and managing a creator or brand's Threads presence.
access_note: Developers create a Meta app and obtain user access tokens via the OAuth 2.0 authorization flow; no paid plan required.
api_breadth: full CRUD on Threads posts and user content

name: SendGrid
description: SendGrid provides an email delivery service with a REST API for sending emails and managing marketing resources.
access_note: Sign up for a free SendGrid account and create an API key in Settings → API Keys.
api_breadth: full CRUD on most core objects (excluding billing & email validation)

name: Shopify
description: Shopify provides APIs to manage e‑commerce stores, including products, orders, customers, inventory and checkout.
access_note: Create a custom app in the Dev Dashboard or with Shopify CLI, install it on a store, and the installation generates an access token.
api_breadth: full CRUD on core e‑commerce objects

name: WooCommerce
description: WooCommerce adds e‑commerce functionality to WordPress sites, enabling online stores.
access_note: Generate a consumer key and secret in WooCommerce Settings → Advanced → REST API and use them in API calls.
api_breadth: full CRUD on core e‑commerce objects (products, orders, customers, etc.)

name: BigCommerce
description: BigCommerce provides a SaaS ecommerce platform with REST and GraphQL APIs to manage store data such as products, orders, customers, and storefront experiences.
access_note: Sign up for a free trial sandbox, then go to Settings > API Accounts in the store control panel to generate client_id, client_secret and an access token.
api_breadth: full CRUD on core ecommerce objects (products, orders, customers, etc.)

name: Salesforce Commerce Cloud
description: Salesforce Commerce Cloud provides APIs to build and customize headless e‑commerce storefronts.
access_note: Requires a B2C Commerce license (realm); API clients are issued via Account Manager, shopper APIs use SLAS OAuth2.
api_breadth: unknown
blocker: Commerce Cloud license required — no self-serve path
revision_note: corrected in verification fix pass (see verification.json pass1)

name: Magento (Adobe Commerce)
description: Adobe Commerce (Magento) provides an e‑commerce platform with APIs for managing products, orders, customers and storefronts.
access_note: Developers can create API access keys (app ID/secret) in the Marketplace Developer Portal (production or sandbox) without contacting sales.
api_breadth: full CRUD on core commerce objects

name: Squarespace
description: Squarespace is a website and ecommerce platform that provides APIs to manage commerce features such as products, orders, inventory, and contacts.
access_note: Developers generate API keys from a Squarespace merchant site (requires a paid Commerce Advanced plan or an approved Extension) via Settings → Advanced → Developer API Keys.
api_breadth: full CRUD on core commerce objects (products, orders, inventory, contacts, etc.)
blocker: requires paid Commerce Advanced plan or Extension approval

name: Ecwid
description: Ecwid provides a REST API for managing store data, orders, products, customers and other e‑commerce settings.
access_note: Create a custom app in the Ecwid dashboard to obtain public/secret tokens; API is only available on paid (Venture, Business, Unlimited) plans.
api_breadth: full CRUD on core e‑commerce objects (store profile, products, orders, customers, discounts, shipping, payments, etc.)
blocker: API requires a paid Ecwid plan

name: Gumroad
description: Gumroad provides a platform for creators to sell digital products and offers a REST API to manage products, sales, subscribers, and licenses.
access_note: Create an application at https://gumroad.com/oauth/applications, fill in name & redirect URI, then generate an access token.
api_breadth: read/write on products, sales, subscribers, and license management

name: Amazon Selling Partner
description: Provides REST APIs for Amazon sellers and vendors to programmatically access orders, shipments, payments, catalog, inventory and other eCommerce data.
access_note: Developers must register as a seller, create a developer account and request data‑access via the Solution Provider Portal; approval is required.
api_breadth: full CRUD on core eCommerce objects (orders, inventory, listings, payments, reports)

name: Fanbasis
description: FanBasis provides a payment and subscription platform that lets creators sell products, manage customers, and handle transactions via API.
access_note: Log into the FanBasis dashboard and copy the API key from the API Keys section.
api_breadth: full CRUD on payments, subscriptions, customers, products, discounts, and webhooks

name: DataForSEO
description: DataForSEO provides REST APIs that deliver structured SEO and SEM data such as SERP results, keyword metrics, backlinks, and domain analytics.
access_note: Create a free account and obtain the API login and password from the API Access tab in the dashboard; a free unlimited trial is offered.
api_breadth: broad set of read‑only SEO data endpoints (SERP, keyword, backlinks, etc.)

name: SE Ranking
description: SE Ranking provides SEO and GEO data via a REST API and an MCP server for AI assistants.
access_note: Create an API key in the SE Ranking dashboard after signing up (free trial available) – no sales approval required.
api_breadth: full CRUD on SEO data and project management resources
mcp_note: SE Ranking MCP server at https://api.seranking.com/mcp

name: Ahrefs
description: Provides access to Ahrefs marketing and search data via a REST API for custom integrations and workflows.
access_note: API keys are created in Account settings by workspace owners/admins and are available on paid Ahrefs plans (Lite and above) with a 1‑year lifetime.
api_breadth: read‑only reporting on SEO data plus limited management endpoints
blocker: Requires paid Ahrefs plan to obtain API key

name: MrScraper
description: MrScraper provides a web‑scraping platform with AI‑powered and manual scrapers accessible via an API.
access_note: Create a MrScraper account and generate an API token in the dashboard; no explicit paywall mentioned.
api_breadth: full CRUD on scrapers, runs, and results

name: Apify
description: Apify provides a platform for running web‑scraping and automation Actors via a REST API.
access_note: Create a free Apify account and retrieve a secret API token from the API & Integrations page in the Apify Console.
api_breadth: full CRUD on core platform objects (actors, runs, datasets, key‑value stores, schedules, etc.)

name: Firecrawl
description: Firecrawl provides a REST API to search, scrape, crawl, map and extract web content for AI agents.
access_note: Sign up at firecrawl.dev to obtain an API key, or use the free keyless tier (1,000 credits/month) without a key.
api_breadth: scrape, crawl, map, search, extract, parse, and browser automation endpoints

name: Bright Data
description: Bright Data is a web data platform that helps you collect, process and scale access to public web data.
access_note: Developers can obtain API credentials on a free plan or trial without talking to sales or waiting for approval.
api_breadth: full CRUD on all core objects

name: Sherlock
description: Find usernames across 400+ social networks and websites.
access_note: Download the open‑source tool from GitHub and run it locally; no credentials required.
api_breadth: read‑only username search across many sites

name: Waterfall.io
description: Waterfall.io provides an enrichment API that aggregates data from 30+ vendors to find and enrich contact, email, and phone information for B2B prospects.
access_note: Sign up for a Waterfall account, obtain a master API key from the dashboard, then create sub‑keys via the API‑keys endpoint.
api_breadth: search, enrichment and verification of contacts and companies
blocker: requires master API key, likely a paid account

name: Clay
description: GTM data-enrichment platform that orchestrates 100+ data providers inside spreadsheet-like tables.
access_note: The app is self-serve, but there is no public inbound developer API — Clay's 'HTTP API' column calls OUT to other services, and data gets in via per-table webhooks.
api_breadth: outbound HTTP-API column + per-table inbound webhooks only
blocker: no public inbound API to build against

name: GitHub
description: GitHub is a DevOps platform for version control and collaboration on code.
access_note: Developers can get free access to the API, but some features require authentication tokens, which can be obtained through personal access tokens, GitHub Apps, or GitHub Actions workflows.
api_breadth: full CRUD on all core objects

name: Vercel
description: Frontend cloud platform for deploying and hosting web apps with serverless infrastructure.
access_note: Create an access token in dashboard Account Settings > Tokens on the free Hobby plan.
api_breadth: full platform control: deployments, projects, domains, DNS, env vars, teams

name: Netlify
description: Web development platform for building, deploying and hosting sites with serverless functions.
access_note: Personal access token from User Settings > Applications on the free tier; OAuth2 for public integrations.
api_breadth: sites, deploys, DNS, forms, functions, env vars

name: Cloudflare
description: CDN, DNS, security and edge-compute platform fronting a large share of the web.
access_note: Scoped API tokens created in the dashboard on the free plan (legacy global API key also exists).
api_breadth: very broad: zones, DNS, Workers, R2, KV, firewall, analytics

name: Supabase
description: Supabase is a full Postgres database for every project with Realtime functionality, database backups, extensions, and more.
access_note: Developers can get credentials by signing up for a free plan or trial without talking to sales or waiting for approval.
api_breadth: full CRUD on all core objects

name: Neo4j
description: Neo4j Aura is a fully automated graph platform offered as a cloud service.
access_note: Developers can obtain credentials through the Aura console, but some features require a paid plan or admin approval.
api_breadth: full CRUD on all core objects

name: Snowflake
description: Cloud data warehouse for analytics, data engineering and AI workloads.
access_note: 30-day free trial account; SQL API authenticates with OAuth or key-pair JWT, programmatic access tokens also supported.
api_breadth: SQL API executes any SQL; separate REST APIs for management
blocker: none (account + warehouse setup is the only friction)

name: MongoDB Atlas
description: The MongoDB Atlas Administration API allows developers to manage all components in MongoDB Atlas.
access_note: Developers can create service accounts or API keys for an organization, but require Organization Owner access to create a service account or API keys for an organization, and Project Owner access to grant API keys access to a project.
api_breadth: full CRUD on all core objects
blocker: Organization Owner access required for service accounts and Project Owner access required for API keys

name: Datadog
description: Observability platform: infrastructure monitoring, APM, logs and security.
access_note: API key + application key generated in Organization Settings on a free trial (free tier keeps limited features).
api_breadth: very broad: metrics, monitors, dashboards, logs, incidents, users

name: Sentry
description: Error tracking and application performance monitoring for developers.
access_note: Organization or personal auth tokens created in settings on the free developer plan; OAuth for public integrations.
api_breadth: projects, issues, events, releases, alerts, teams

name: Notion
description: All-in-one workspace for notes, docs, wikis and databases.
access_note: Create an internal integration at notion.so/my-integrations on any plan (including free) for an instant token; OAuth2 for public integrations.
api_breadth: pages, databases, blocks, users, comments, search

name: Airtable
description: Airtable is a low-code platform for building collaborative apps.
access_note: Developers can obtain credentials by creating a Personal Access Token on the Airtable dashboard.
api_breadth: full CRUD on all core objects
revision_note: corrected in verification fix pass (see verification.json pass1)

name: Linear
description: A project management tool for teams.
access_note: Developers can create an account and generate API keys or use OAuth2 for authentication.
api_breadth: full CRUD on all core objects
mcp_note: Model Context Protocol (MCP) server provided by Linear

name: Jira
description: Jira is a productivity and project management app.
access_note: Developers can get credentials by creating an API token, but some features require a paid plan or admin approval.
api_breadth: full CRUD on all core objects
blocker: Migrate from API tokens to officially supported authentication by December 31st, 2025

name: Asana
description: Asana is a productivity and project management app.
access_note: Developers can obtain credentials by registering their app and generating a personal access token.
api_breadth: full CRUD on all core objects

name: Monday.com
description: A productivity and project management platform that allows teams to create and shape their own workflows in minutes, code-free.
access_note: Developers can obtain API tokens via the Developers section, following these steps: Log into their monday.com account, click on their profile picture, select Developers, and click API token > Show.
api_breadth: full CRUD on all core objects
mcp_note: monday MCP remote server connects via the secure OAuth protocol.

name: ClickUp
description: Productivity and project management tool for teams.
access_note: Developers can access credentials by creating an account, but some features require a paid plan or admin approval.
api_breadth: full CRUD on core objects
blocker: OAuth app creation requires Workspace owner or admin approval

name: Coda
description: Coda is an all-in-one collaborative workspace that blends the flexibility of docs, structure of spreadsheets, power of applications, and intelligence of AI.
access_note: API key can be obtained in the Coda account, but API usage is subject to the role of the user associated with the API token.
api_breadth: full CRUD on all core objects
blocker: API usage is subject to the role of the user associated with the API token

name: Smartsheet
description: The Smartsheet app is a productivity and project management tool that enables users to programmatically access and manage their organization's Smartsheet resources.
access_note: Developers can generate an API access token in the Smartsheet UI, but tokens generated for Smartsheet, Smartsheet Regions instances, and Smartsheet Gov are not interchangeable.
api_breadth: full CRUD on core objects
blocker: token generation process varies by environment

name: Harvest
description: Time tracking and invoicing for teams and freelancers.
access_note: Personal access token from id.getharvest.com/developers on the free plan or 30-day trial; OAuth2 for multi-account apps.
api_breadth: time entries, projects, clients, invoices, estimates, reports

name: Stripe
description: Payments infrastructure: accept, process and manage online payments.
access_note: Instant test-mode API keys on signup; live keys after activating the account. OAuth2 for Connect platforms.
api_breadth: very broad: payments, customers, subscriptions, invoices, payouts, issuing

name: Plaid
description: Plaid is a financial services company that provides APIs for accessing financial data.
access_note: Create an account on the Plaid Dashboard to obtain API credentials.
api_breadth: full CRUD on all core objects

name: Binance
description: The Binance app provides access to cryptocurrency market data and trading functionality.
access_note: Developers can create API keys on their Binance account, but some features may require a paid plan or approval.
api_breadth: full CRUD on all core objects
mcp_note: Binance MCP Server: LLM Access to Crypto Data

name: Paygent Connect
description: Paygent Connect is a payment orchestration platform that enables AI agents to integrate with payment gateways.
access_note: Developers can obtain API credentials through a free trial or a paid plan, but may need to contact sales for enterprise access.
api_breadth: full CRUD on payment gateways
mcp_note: earlier 'official' claim cited a page that does not mention MCP
revision_note: corrected in verification fix pass (see verification.json pass1)

name: iPayX
description: iPayX is a FINTRAC-registered FX forensic audit layer.
access_note: Access credentials through the iPayX API key management interface, which requires administrator privileges.
api_breadth: full CRUD on FX audit data
blocker: API key management interface requires administrator privileges

name: QuickBooks
description: QuickBooks is a finance and fintech app for accounting and bookkeeping.
access_note: Developers can get credentials by creating an Intuit Developer Account, but may need a paid plan or admin approval.
api_breadth: full CRUD on all core objects

name: Xero
description: Xero is a cloud-based accounting software that allows users to manage their finances, invoicing, and accounting.
access_note: Sign up for a free Xero account to get credentials, no paywall or approval required.
api_breadth: full CRUD on all core objects

name: Brex
description: Brex is a financial platform that allows developers to integrate with their banking services.
access_note: To get API access to Brex, visit their developer portal at developer.brex.com, create a developer account, review the API documentation and terms of service, register your application to obtain API credentials, and test your integration in the sandbox environment before going live.
api_breadth: full CRUD on all core objects

name: Ramp
description: Ramp is a finance and fintech app that provides a platform for businesses to manage their expenses and finances.
access_note: Developers need admin access to create credentials and make API calls, but there is a free trial available.
api_breadth: full CRUD on all core objects

name: PitchBook
description: PitchBook provides real-time access to company data and market intelligence for financial workflows.
access_note: The API is a separate offering from the PitchBook platform that requires a standalone contract agreement — sales-gated.
api_breadth: full CRUD on company data and market intelligence
blocker: standalone contract with PitchBook sales required
revision_note: corrected after human docs check (see manual_checks.json)

name: NotebookLM
description: Google's AI research notebook that grounds answers in user-uploaded sources.
access_note: No API for the consumer product; the API exists only in NotebookLM Enterprise via Google Cloud (Gemini Enterprise/Agentspace licensing + IAM roles).
api_breadth: Enterprise-only: create/manage notebooks and sources via Discovery Engine API
blocker: enterprise-only API behind Gemini Enterprise licensing

name: Otter AI
description: AI meeting transcription and notetaking assistant.
access_note: A public API key can be created under Integrations > Developer in account settings; the Otter MCP server is limited to Business/Enterprise plans.
api_breadth: speeches/transcripts export and search (narrow)
blocker: API is new and narrow; MCP gated to Business/Enterprise
researched_by: human (Claude fallback); help-center pages block bots (403), claims based on page snippets

name: Fathom
description: Fathom captures, transcribes, and summarizes meetings, and provides a public API for accessing meeting insights.
access_note: Developers can generate an API key and make their first call in minutes.
api_breadth: read and write access to meeting data
mcp_note: earlier hit (mcp-fathom-analytics) is for Fathom Analytics, a different product
revision_note: corrected after human docs check (see manual_checks.json)

name: Consensus
description: Consensus is an AI academic search engine for peer-reviewed literature that helps find, organize, and analyze science 10x faster.
access_note: Apply today to receive a custom quote tailored to your team's needs, and access is available by application only.
api_breadth: full CRUD on all core objects

name: Reducto
description: Document-parsing API platform that turns PDFs and scans into structured data for AI pipelines.
access_note: Sign up to Reducto Studio and 'parse your first document in 5 minutes' via the API quickstart; enterprise tier adds dedicated support.
api_breadth: parse, extract, split, edit pipelines
mcp_note: Reducto MCP tools for Claude Code/Cursor per docs

name: Devin
description: Cognition's autonomous AI software engineer that plans and executes coding tasks.
access_note: API uses service-user credentials (cog_ prefix) as Bearer tokens; requires a paid Devin subscription to have an account at all.
api_breadth: sessions, playbooks, knowledge, schedules

name: Higgsfield
description: Generate and manage AI images and videos with Higgsfield's unified model API.
access_note: Developers can sign up for a free account, but some features require a paid plan or admin approval.
api_breadth: full CRUD on all core objects
mcp_note: Model Context Protocol (MCP) is an open standard that gives AI agents secure access to external tools.

name: Mermaid CLI
description: A command-line interface (CLI) for mermaid that takes a mermaid definition file as input and generates an SVG/PNG/PDF file as output.
access_note: Open-source npm CLI (mmdc); no credentials of any kind — runs locally.
api_breadth: diagram file in, SVG/PNG/PDF out; also a Node.js run() API
blocker: no hosted API — a toolkit would wrap the local CLI
revision_note: corrected after human docs check (see manual_checks.json)

name: YouTube Transcript
description: Third-party REST API (transcriptapi.com) for fetching YouTube transcripts, video and channel data.
access_note: Sign up and get an API key immediately with 100 free credits, no card required; paid from ~$5/month.
api_breadth: transcripts, video search, channels, playlists

name: Grain
description: AI meeting recorder that captures, transcribes and shares video call highlights.
access_note: Personal access token generated in app settings (grain.com/app/settings/integrations); OAuth2 with PKCE for public apps; docs don't state plan gating.
api_breadth: recordings, transcripts, webhooks, users, teams
